message:
feat(combinator): add AdaptiveHedgePolicy with conformal prediction

New AdaptiveHedgePolicy in hedge.rs uses Marginal Conformal Prediction
to dynamically calibrate hedge delays based on a sliding window of
recent primary execution latencies. Under exchangeability, this
provides a finite-sample guarantee that hedges fire only on true
statistical outliers.

Key design:
- Sliding window of u64 microsecond latencies (configurable size)
- conformal_rank() computes ceiling((n+1)*(1-alpha)) for the
  (1-alpha) quantile rank (e.g. P95 at alpha=0.05)
- next_hedge_delay() sorts the window, picks the quantile, and
  clamps between min_delay and max_delay
- Falls back to max_delay when fewer than 10 observations exist
- record() updates the circular buffer for ongoing calibration

New adaptive_hedge module registered in combinator/mod.rs with
PeakEwmaHedgeController re-export.

Tests:
- test_adaptive_hedge_policy_conformal_quantile: verifies exact P95
  quantile computation with 100 sequential latencies
- test_adaptive_hedge_policy_clamps: verifies min/max clamping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
